question: Which of the following specialties might focus on studying all of the structures of the ankle and foot?
A scientist wants to study how the body uses foods and fluids during a marathon run. This scientist is most likely a(n) ________.
The smallest independently functioning biological unit of an organism is a(n) ________.
A collection of similar tissues that performs a specific function is an ________.
The body system responsible for structural support and movement is the ________.
Metabolism can be defined as the ________.
choices: adjustment by an organism to external or internal changes
process whereby all unspecialized cells become specialized to perform distinct functions
process whereby new cells are formed to replace worn-out cells
sum of all chemical reactions in an organism

question: Adenosine triphosphate (ATP) is an important molecule because it ________.
choices: stores energy for use by body cells

question: Cancer cells can be characterized as “generic” cells that perform no specialized body function. Thus cancer cells lack ________.
Humans have the most urgent need for a continuous supply of ________.
Which of the following statements about nutrients is true?
choices: All classes of nutrients are essential to human survival.
Because the body cannot store any micronutrients, they need to be consumed nearly every day.
Carbohydrates, lipids, and proteins are micronutrients.

question: C.J. is stuck in their car during a bitterly cold blizzard. Their body responds to the cold by ________.
choices: increasing the blood to the hands and feet

question: After you eat lunch, nerve cells in your stomach respond to the distension (the stimulus) resulting from the food. They relay this information to ________.
Stimulation of the heat-loss center causes ________.
choices: blood vessels in the skin to constrict
breathing to become slow and shallow
sweat glands to increase their output

question: Which of the following is an example of a normal physiologic process that uses a positive feedback loop?
What is the position of the body when it is in the “normal anatomical position?”
choices: The person is prone with upper limbs, including palms, touching sides and lower limbs touching at sides.
The person is standing facing the observer, with upper limbs extended out at a ninety-degree angle from the torso and lower limbs in a wide stance with feet pointing laterally
The person is supine with upper limbs, including palms, touching sides and lower limbs touching at sides.

question: To make a banana split, you halve a banana into two long, thin, right and left sides along the ________.
The lumbar region is ________.
The heart is within the ________.
In 1901, Wilhelm Röntgen was the first person to win the Nobel Prize for physics. For what discovery did he win?
choices: the link between radiation and cancer

question: Which of the following imaging techniques would be best to use to study the uptake of nutrients by rapidly multiplying cancer cells?
Which of the following imaging studies can be used most safely during pregnancy?
What are two major disadvantages of MRI scans?
choices: release of radiation and poor quality images
high cost and the need for shielding from the magnetic signals
can only view metabolically active tissues and inadequate availability of equipment
release of radiation and the need for a patient to be confined to metal tube for up to 30 minutes

question: Together, just four elements make up more than 95 percent of the body’s mass. These include ________.
The smallest unit of an element that still retains the distinctive behavior of that element is an ________.
The characteristic that gives an element its distinctive properties is its number of ________.
On the periodic table of the elements, mercury (Hg) has an atomic number of 80 and a mass number of 200.59. It has seven stable isotopes. The most abundant of these probably have ________.
Nitrogen has an atomic number of seven. How many electron shells does it likely have?
Which of the following is a molecule, but not a compound?
A molecule of ammonia contains one atom of nitrogen and three atoms of hydrogen. These are linked with ________.
When an atom donates an electron to another atom, it becomes
A substance formed of crystals of equal numbers of cations and anions held together by ionic bonds is called a(n) ________.
Which of the following statements about chemical bonds is true?
choices: Covalent bonds are stronger than ionic bonds in biological systems.
Hydrogen bonds occur between two atoms of hydrogen.
Bonding readily occurs between nonpolar and polar molecules.
A molecule of water is unlikely to bond with an ion.

question: The energy stored in a foot of snow on a steep roof is ________.
The bonding of calcium, phosphorus, and other elements produces mineral crystals that are found in bone. This is an example of a(n) ________ reaction.
AB→A+B is a general notation for a(n) ________ reaction.
________ reactions release energy.
Which of the following combinations of atoms is most likely to result in a chemical reaction?
Chewing a bite of bread mixes it with saliva and facilitates its chemical breakdown. This is most likely due to the fact that ________.
choices: the inside of the mouth maintains a very high temperature

question: CH4 is methane. This compound is ________.
Which of the following is most likely to be found evenly distributed in water in a homogeneous solution?

Jenny mixes up a batch of pancake batter, then stirs in some chocolate chips. As she is waiting for the first few pancakes to cook, she notices the chocolate chips sinking to the bottom of the clear glass mixing bowl. The chocolate-chip batter is an example of a ________.
A substance dissociates into K+ and Cl– in solution. The substance is a(n) ________.
Ty is three years old and as a result of a “stomach bug” has been vomiting for about 24 hours. His blood pH is 7.48. What does this mean?
choices: Ty’s blood is within the normal range

question: C6H12O6 is the chemical formula for a ________.
What organic compound do brain cells primarily rely on for fuel?
Which of the following is a functional group that is part of a building block of proteins?
A pentose sugar is a part of the monomer used to build which type of macromolecule?
A phospholipid ________.
choices: has both polar and nonpolar regions
is made up of a triglyceride bonded to a phosphate group
is a building block of ATP
can donate both cations and anions in solution

question: In DNA, nucleotide bonding forms a compound with a characteristic shape known as a(n) ________.
Uracil ________.
The ability of an enzyme’s active sites to bind only substrates of compatible shape and charge is known as ________.
Because they are embedded within the membrane, ion channels are examples of ________.
The diffusion of substances within a solution tends to move those substances ________ their ________ gradient.
Ion pumps and phagocytosis are both examples of ________.
Choose the answer that best completes the following analogy: Diffusion is to ________ as endocytosis is to ________.
Choose the term that best completes the following analogy: Cytoplasm is to cytosol as a swimming pool containing chlorine and flotation toys is to ________.
The rough ER has its name due to what associated structures?
Which of the following is a function of the rough ER?
Which of the following is a feature common to all three components of the cytoskeleton?
choices: They all serve to scaffold the organelles within the cell.
They are all characterized by roughly the same diameter.
They are all polymers of protein subunits.
They all help the cell resist compression and tension.

question: Which of the following organelles produces large quantities of ATP when both glucose and oxygen are available to the cell?
The nucleus and mitochondria share which of the following features?
Which of the following structures could be found within the nucleolus?
Which of the following sequences on a DNA molecule would be complementary to GCTTATAT?
Place the following structures in order from least to most complex organization: chromatin, nucleosome, DNA, chromosome
Which of the following is part of the elongation step of DNA synthesis?
choices: pulling apart the two DNA strands
attaching complementary nucleotides to the template strand

question: Which of the following is not a difference between DNA and RNA?
choices: DNA contains thymine whereas RNA contains uracil
DNA contains deoxyribose and RNA contains ribose
DNA contains alternating sugar-phosphate molecules whereas RNA does not contain sugars
RNA is single stranded and DNA is double stranded

question: Transcription and translation take place in the ________ and ________, respectively.
How many “letters” of an RNA molecule, in sequence, does it take to provide the code for a single amino acid?
Which of the following is not entirely or mostly made out of RNA?
choices: the carriers that shuttle amino acids to a growing polypeptide strand
the messenger molecule that provides the code for protein synthesis

question: Which of the following phases is characterized by preparation for DNA synthesis?
A mutation in the gene for a cyclin protein might result in which of the following?
choices: a cell with additional genetic material than normal
a cell with less genetic material than normal

question: What is a primary function of tumor suppressor genes?
choices: allow the cell to skip certain phases of the cell cycle

question: Arrange the following terms in order of increasing specialization: oligopotency, pleuripotency, unipotency, multipotency.
Which type of stem cell gives rise to red and white blood cells?
What multipotent stem cells from children sometimes banked by parents?
choices: cells from the umbilical cord and from baby teeth
hematopoietic stem cells from red and white blood cells

question: Which of the following is not a type of tissue?
The process by which a less specialized cell matures into a more specialized cell is called ________.
Differentiated cells in a developing embryo derive from ________.
choices: connective tissue, epithelial tissue, and muscle tissue

question: Which of the following lines the body cavities exposed to the external environment?

In observing epithelial cells under a microscope, the cells are arranged in a single layer and look tall and narrow, and the nucleus is located close to the basal side of the cell. The specimen is what type of epithelial tissue?
Which of the following is the epithelial tissue that lines the interior of blood vessels?
Which type of epithelial tissue specializes in moving particles across its surface?
The ________ exocrine gland stores its secretion until the glandular cell ruptures, whereas the ________ gland releases its apical region and reforms.
Connective tissue is made of which three essential components?
choices: cells, ground substance, and carbohydrate fibers
cells, ground substance, and protein fibers
collagen, ground substance, and protein fibers

question: Under the microscope, a tissue specimen shows cells located in spaces scattered in a transparent background. This is probably ________.
Which connective tissue specializes in storage of fat?
Ligaments connect bones together and withstand a lot of stress. What type of connective tissue should you expect ligaments to contain?
In adults, new connective tissue cells originate from the ________.
In bone, the main cells are ________.
Striations, cylindrical cells, and multiple nuclei are observed in ________.
The cells of muscles, myocytes, develop from ________.
Skeletal muscle is composed of very hard working cells. Which organelles do you expect to find in abundance in skeletal muscle cell?
The cells responsible for the transmission of the nerve impulse are ________.
The nerve impulse travels down a(n) ________, away from the cell body.
Which of the following central nervous system cells regulate ions, regulate the uptake and/or breakdown of some neurotransmitters, and contribute to the formation of the blood-brain barrier?
Which of the following processes is not a cardinal sign of inflammation?
When a mast cell reacts to an irritation, which of the following chemicals does it release?
Atrophy refers to ________.
Individuals can slow the rate of aging by modifying all of these lifestyle aspects except for ________.
The papillary layer of the dermis is most closely associated with which layer of the epidermis?
Langerhans cells are commonly found in the ________.
The papillary and reticular layers of the dermis are composed mainly of ________.
Collagen lends ________ to the skin.
Which of the following is not a function of the hypodermis?
choices: source of blood vessels in the epidermis
a site to long-term energy storage

question: In response to stimuli from the sympathetic nervous system, the arrector pili ________.
choices: are glands on the skin surface

question: The hair matrix contains ________.
Eccrine sweat glands ________.
choices: are present in the skin throughout the body and produce watery sweat

question: Sebaceous glands ________.
choices: are a type of sweat gland
may function in response to touch
release a watery solution of salt and metabolic waste

question: Similar to the hair, nails grow continuously throughout our lives. Which of the following is furthest from the nail growth center?
In humans, exposure of the skin to sunlight is required for ________.
One of the functions of the integumentary system is protection. Which of the following does not directly contribute to that function?
An individual using a sharp knife notices a small amount of blood where he just cut himself. Which of the following layers of skin did he have to cut into in order to bleed?
As you are walking down the beach, you see a dead, dry, shriveled-up fish. Which layer of your epidermis keeps you from drying out?
If you cut yourself and bacteria enter the wound, which of the following cells would help get rid of the bacteria?
In general, skin cancers ________.
choices: are easily treatable and not a major health concern
can be reduced by limiting exposure to the sun

question: Bedsores ________.
choices: can be treated with topical moisturizers
are preventable by eliminating pressure points

question: An individual has spent too much time sun bathing. Not only is their skin painful to touch, but small blisters have appeared in the affected area. This indicates that they have damaged which layers of skin?
After a skin injury, the body initiates a wound-healing response. The first step of this response is the formation of a blood clot to stop bleeding. Which of the following would be the next response?
choices: increased production of melanin by melanocytes
an increase in Pacinian corpuscles around the wound
an increased activity in the stratum lucidum

question: Squamous cell carcinomas are the second most common of the skin cancers and are capable of metastasizing if not treated. This cancer affects which cells?
choices: basal cells of the stratum basale
Langerhans cells of the stratum lucidum

question: Which function of the skeletal system would be especially important if you were in a car accident?
Bone tissue can be described as ________.
Without red marrow, bones would not be able to ________.
Yellow marrow has been identified as ________.
choices: a point of attachment for muscles

question: Which of the following can be found in areas of movement?
The skeletal system is made of ________.
Most of the bones of the arms and hands are long bones; however, the bones in the wrist are categorized as ________.
Sesamoid bones are found embedded in ________.
Bones that surround the spinal cord are classified as ________ bones.
Which category of bone is among the most numerous in the skeleton?
Long bones enable body movement by acting as a ________.
Which of the following occurs in the spongy bone of the epiphysis?
The diaphysis contains ________.
The fibrous membrane covering the outer surface of the bone is the ________.
Which of the following cells are capable of undergoing mitosis?
Which cells do not originate from osteogenic cells?
Which of the following are found in compact bone and cancellous bone?
Which of the following are only found in cancellous bone?
The area of a bone where the nutrient foramen passes forms what kind of bone marking?
Why is cartilage slow to heal?
choices: because it eventually develops into bone
because it is semi-solid and flexible
because it does not have a blood supply
because endochondral ossification replaces all cartilage with bone

question: Why are osteocytes spread out in bone tissue?
choices: Formation of osteoid spreads out the osteoblasts that formed the ossification centers.

question: In endochondral ossification, what happens to the chondrocytes?
choices: They die in the calcified matrix that surrounds them and form the medullary cavity.
They grow and form the periosteum.
They group together to form the primary ossification center.

question: Which of the following bones is (are) formed by intramembranous ossification?
choices: the flat bones of the cranium

question: Bones grow in length due to activity in the ________.
Bones grow in diameter due to bone formation ________.
Which of the following represents the correct sequence of zones in the epiphyseal plate?
A fracture can be both ________.
How can a fractured diaphysis release fat globules into the bloodstream?
choices: The bone pierces fat stores in the skin.
The yellow marrow in the diaphysis is exposed and damaged.
The injury triggers the body to release fat from healthy bones.
The red marrow in the fractured bone releases fat to heal the fracture.

question: In a compound fracture, ________.
choices: the break occurs at an angle to the bone
the broken bone does not tear the skin
one fragment of broken bone is compressed into the other

question: The internal and external calli are replaced by ________.
The first type of bone to form during fracture repair is ________ bone.
Wolff’s law, which describes the effect of mechanical forces in bone modeling/remodeling, would predict that ________
choices: a right-handed pitcher will have thicker bones in his right arm compared to his left.
a right-handed cyclist will have thicker bones in her right leg compared to her left.
a broken bone will heal thicker than it was before the fracture.
a bed-ridden patient will have thicker bones than an athlete.

question: Calcium cannot be absorbed from the small intestine if ________ is lacking.
Which one of the following foods is best for bone health?
Which of the following hormones are responsible for the adolescent growth spurt?
With respect to their direct effects on osseous tissue, which pair of hormones has actions that oppose each other?
When calcium levels are too high or too low, which body system is primarily affected?
All of the following play a role in calcium homeostasis except
Which of the following is most likely to be released when blood calcium levels are elevated?
Which of the following is part of the axial skeleton?
Which of the following is a function of the axial skeleton?
choices: allows for movement of the wrist and hand
protects nerves and blood vessels at the elbow
allows for movements of the ankle and foot

question: The axial skeleton ________.
choices: forms the vertical axis of the body
includes all bones of the body trunk and limbs
includes only the bones of the lower limbs

question: Which of the following is a bone of the brain case?
The lambdoid suture joins the parietal bone to the ________.
The middle cranial fossa ________.
choices: is bounded anteriorly by the petrous ridge
is bounded posteriorly by the lesser wing of the sphenoid bone
is divided at the midline by a small area of the ethmoid bone
has the foramen rotundum, foramen ovale, and foramen spinosum

question: The paranasal sinuses are ________.
choices: air-filled spaces found within the frontal, maxilla, sphenoid, and ethmoid bones only
air-filled spaces found within all bones of the skull
not connected to the nasal cavity
divided at the midline by the nasal septum

question: Parts of the sphenoid bone include the ________.
The bony openings of the skull include the ________.
choices: carotid canal, which is located in the anterior cranial fossa
superior orbital fissure, which is located at the superior margin of the anterior orbit
mental foramen, which is located just below the orbit
hypoglossal canal, which is located in the posterior cranial fossa

question: The cervical region of the vertebral column consists of ________.
choices: a single bone derived from the fusion of five vertebrae

question: The primary curvatures of the vertebral column ________.
choices: are remnants of the original fetal curvature
develop after the time of birth

question: A typical vertebra has ________.
choices: a vertebral foramen that passes through the body
a superior articular process that projects downward to articulate with the superior portion of the next lower vertebra
lamina that spans between the transverse process and spinous process
a pair of laterally projecting spinous processes

question: A typical lumbar vertebra has ________.
Which is found only in the cervical region of the vertebral column?
The sternum ________.
choices: consists of only two parts, the manubrium and xiphoid process
has the sternal angle located between the manubrium and body
receives direct attachments from the costal cartilages of all 12 pairs of ribs
articulates directly with the thoracic vertebrae

question: The sternal angle is the ________.
choices: junction between the body and xiphoid process
site for attachment of the clavicle
site for attachment of the floating ribs
junction between the manubrium and body

question: The tubercle of a rib ________.
choices: is for articulation with the transverse process of a thoracic vertebra
is for articulation with the body of a thoracic vertebra
provides for passage of blood vessels and a nerve
is the area of greatest rib curvature

question: True ribs are ________.
choices: attached via their costal cartilage to the next higher rib
made entirely of bone, and thus do not have a costal cartilage
attached via their costal cartilage directly to the sternum

question: Embryonic development of the axial skeleton involves ________.
choices: intramembranous ossification, which forms the facial bones.
endochondral ossification, which forms the ribs and sternum
the notochord, which produces the cartilage models for the vertebrae
the formation of hyaline cartilage models, which give rise to the flat bones of the skull

question: A fontanelle ________.
choices: is the cartilage model for a vertebra that later is converted into bone
gives rise to the facial bones and vertebrae
is the rod-like structure that runs the length of the early embryo
is the area of fibrous connective tissue found at birth between the brain case bones

question: Which part of the clavicle articulates with the manubrium?
A shoulder separation results from injury to the ________.
Which feature lies between the spine and superior border of the scapula?
What structure is an extension of the spine of the scapula?
Name the short, hook-like bony process of the scapula that projects anteriorly.
How many bones are there in the upper limbs combined?
Which bony landmark is located on the lateral side of the proximal humerus?
Which region of the humerus articulates with the radius as part of the elbow joint?
Which is the lateral-most carpal bone of the proximal row?
The radius bone ________.
choices: is found on the medial side of the forearm
has a head that articulates with the radial notch of the ulna
does not articulate with any of the carpal bones
has the radial tuberosity located near its distal end

question: How many bones fuse in adulthood to form the hip bone?
Which component forms the superior part of the hip bone?
Which of the following supports body weight when sitting?
The ischial spine is found between which of the following structures?
choices: inferior pubic ramus and ischial ramus
lesser sciatic notch and greater sciatic notch
anterior superior iliac spine and posterior superior iliac spine

question: The pelvis ________.
choices: has a subpubic angle that is larger in females
consists of the two hip bones, but does not include the sacrum or coccyx
has an obturator foramen, an opening that is defined in part by the sacrospinous and sacrotuberous ligaments
has a space located inferior to the pelvic brim called the greater pelvis

question: Which bony landmark of the femur serves as a site for muscle attachments?
What structure contributes to the knee joint?
Which tarsal bone articulates with the tibia and fibula?
What is the total number of bones found in the foot and toes?
The tibia ________.
choices: has an expanded distal end called the lateral malleolus
is firmly anchored to the fibula by an interosseous membrane
can be palpated (felt) under the skin only at its proximal and distal ends

question: Which event takes place during the seventh week of development?
choices: appearance of the upper and lower limb buds
flattening of the distal limb bud into a paddle shape
the first appearance of hyaline cartilage models of future bones

question: During endochondral ossification of a long bone, ________.
choices: a primary ossification center will develop within the epiphysis
mesenchyme will differentiate directly into bone tissue
growth of the epiphyseal plate will produce bone lengthening
all epiphyseal plates will disappear before birth

question: The clavicle ________.
choices: is the last bone of the body to begin ossification
is fully ossified at the time of birth

question: The joint between adjacent vertebrae that includes an invertebral disc is classified as which type of joint?
Which of these joints is classified as a synarthrosis?
Which of these joints is classified as a biaxial diarthrosis?
Synovial joints ________.
choices: may be functionally classified as a synarthrosis
are joints where the bones are connected to each other by hyaline cartilage
may be functionally classified as an amphiarthrosis
are joints where the bones articulate with each other within a fluid-filled joint cavity

question: Which type of fibrous joint connects the tibia and fibula?
An example of a wide fibrous joint is ________.
choices: the interosseous membrane of the forearm

question: A gomphosis ________.
choices: is formed by an interosseous membrane
connects the tibia and fibula bones of the leg
anchors a tooth to the jaw

question: A syndesmosis is ________.
choices: the type of joint that unites bones of the skull
a fibrous joint that unites parallel bones
the type of joint that anchors the teeth in the jaws

question: A cartilaginous joint ________.
choices: is called a symphysis when the bones are united by fibrocartilage
anchors the teeth to the jaws
is formed by a wide sheet of fibrous connective tissue

question: A synchondrosis is ________.
choices: where bones are connected together with fibrocartilage
found at the first sternocostal joint of the thoracic cage

question: Which of the following are joined by a symphysis?
choices: the first rib and the sternum
the end and shaft of a long bone

question: The epiphyseal plate of a growing long bone in a child is classified as a ________.
Which type of joint provides the greatest range of motion?
Which type of joint allows for only uniaxial movement?
Which of the following is a type of synovial joint?
A bursa ________.
choices: surrounds a tendon at the point where the tendon crosses a joint
secretes the lubricating fluid for a synovial joint
prevents friction between skin and bone, or a muscle tendon and bone
is the strong band of connective tissue that holds bones together at a synovial joint

question: At synovial joints, ________.
choices: the articulating ends of the bones are directly connected by fibrous connective tissue
the ends of the bones are enclosed within a space called a subcutaneous bursa
intrinsic ligaments are located entirely inside of the articular capsule
the joint cavity is filled with a thick, lubricating fluid

question: At a synovial joint, the synovial membrane ________.
choices: forms the fibrous connective walls of the joint cavity
is the layer of cartilage that covers the articulating surfaces of the bones

question: Condyloid joints ________.
choices: are a type of ball-and-socket joint
are found at the proximal radioulnar joint

question: A meniscus is ________.
choices: a fibrocartilage pad that provides padding between bones
a fluid-filled space that prevents friction between a muscle tendon and underlying bone
the articular cartilage that covers the ends of a bone at a synovial joint
the lubricating fluid within a synovial joint

question: The joints between the articular processes of adjacent vertebrae can contribute to which movement?
Which motion moves the bottom of the foot away from the midline of the body?
Movement of a body region in a circular movement at a condyloid joint is what type of motion?
Supination is the motion that moves the ________.
choices: hand from the palm backward position to the palm forward position
foot so that the bottom of the foot faces the midline of the body
hand from the palm forward position to the palm backward position

question: Movement at the shoulder joint that moves the upper limb laterally away from the body is called ________.
The primary support for the glenohumeral joint is provided by the ________.
The proximal radioulnar joint ________.
choices: is supported by the annular ligament
contains an articular disc that strongly unites the bones
is supported by the ulnar collateral ligament
is a hinge joint that allows for flexion/extension of the forearm

question: Which statement is true concerning the knee joint?
choices: The lateral meniscus is an intrinsic ligament located on the lateral side of the knee joint.
Hyperextension is resisted by the posterior cruciate ligament.
The anterior cruciate ligament supports the knee when it is flexed and weight bearing.
The medial meniscus is attached to the tibial collateral ligament.

question: The ankle joint ________.
choices: is also called the subtalar joint
allows for gliding movements that produce inversion/eversion of the foot
is supported by the tibial collateral ligament on the lateral side

question: Which region of the vertebral column has the greatest range of motion for axial rotation?
Intramembranous ossification ________.
choices: gives rise to the bones of the limbs
produces the bones of the top and sides of the skull
produces the bones of the face and base of the skull
involves the conversion of a hyaline cartilage model into bone

question: Synovial joints ________.
Endochondral ossification is ________.
choices: the process that replaces hyaline cartilage with bone tissue
the process by which mesenchyme differentiates directly into bone tissue
the process that gives rise to the joint interzone and future joint cavity

question: Muscle that has a striped appearance is described as being ________.
Which element is important in directly triggering contraction?
Which of the following properties is not common to all three muscle tissues?
choices: at rest, uses shielding proteins to cover actin-binding sites

question: The correct order for the smallest to the largest unit of organization in muscle tissue is ________.
Depolarization of the sarcolemma means ________.
choices: the inside of the membrane has become less negative as sodium ions accumulate
the outside of the membrane has become less negative as sodium ions accumulate
the inside of the membrane has become more negative as sodium ions accumulate
the sarcolemma has completely lost any electrical charge

question: In relaxed muscle, the myosin-binding site on actin is blocked by ________.
According to the sliding filament model, binding sites on actin open when ________.
The cell membrane of a muscle fiber is called ________.
Muscle relaxation occurs when ________.
choices: calcium ions are actively transported out of the sarcoplasmic reticulum
calcium ions diffuse out of the sarcoplasmic reticulum
calcium ions are actively transported into the sarcoplasmic reticulum
calcium ions diffuse into the sarcoplasmic reticulum

question: During muscle contraction, the cross-bridge detaches when ________.
choices: the myosin head binds to an ADP molecule
the myosin head binds to an ATP molecule

question: Thin and thick filaments are organized into functional units called ________.
During which phase of a twitch in a muscle fiber is tension the greatest?
Muscle fatigue is caused by ________.
choices: buildup of ATP and lactic acid levels
exhaustion of energy reserves and buildup of lactic acid levels
buildup of ATP and pyruvic acid levels
exhaustion of energy reserves and buildup of pyruvic acid levels

question: A sprinter would experience muscle fatigue sooner than a marathon runner due to ________.
choices: anaerobic metabolism in the muscles of the sprinter
anaerobic metabolism in the muscles of the marathon runner
aerobic metabolism in the muscles of the sprinter
glycolysis in the muscles of the marathon runner

question: What aspect of creatine phosphate allows it to supply energy to muscles?
Drug X blocks ATP regeneration from ADP and phosphate. How will muscle cells respond to this drug?
choices: by absorbing ATP from the bloodstream
by using ADP as an energy source
by using glycogen as an energy source

question: The muscles of a professional sprinter are most likely to have ________.
choices: 80 percent fast-twitch muscle fibers and 20 percent slow-twitch muscle fibers
20 percent fast-twitch muscle fibers and 80 percent slow-twitch muscle fibers
50 percent fast-twitch muscle fibers and 50 percent slow-twitch muscle fibers
40 percent fast-twitch muscle fibers and 60 percent slow-twitch muscle fibers

question: The muscles of a professional marathon runner are most likely to have ________.
choices: 80 percent fast-twitch muscle fibers and 20 percent slow-twitch muscle fibers
20 percent fast-twitch muscle fibers and 80 percent slow-twitch muscle fibers
50 percent fast-twitch muscle fibers and 50 percent slow-twitch muscle fibers
40 percent fast-twitch muscle fibers and 60 percent slow-twitch muscle fibers

question: Which of the following statements is true?
choices: Fast fibers have a small diameter.
Fast fibers contain loosely packed myofibrils.
Fast fibers have large glycogen reserves.

question: Which of the following statements is false?
choices: Slow fibers have a small network of capillaries.
Slow fibers contain the pigment myoglobin.
